title: Combinatorics
description: Master the art of counting without listing: the multiplication rule, permutations and combinations, the binomial theorem, inclusion-exclusion, the pigeonhole principle, and turning counts into probabilities. From the birthday paradox to poker odds, discover why combinatorial thinking is the key to sizing sets too large to ever enumerate.
persona: For puzzle lovers and quant-interview preppers. No prerequisites beyond comfort with multiplication; each technique is built from scratch through interactive examples.

lessons.title: The Counting Principle
lessons.summary: Multiply independent choices to count outcomes — and arrange a full set with factorials — without ever listing them.

lessons.title: Permutations & Combinations
lessons.summary: Tell ordered selection (nPk) from unordered selection (nCk), and know when order matters.

lessons.title: The Binomial Theorem
lessons.summary: See that the nCk ARE the coefficients of (a+b)ⁿ: Pascal symmetry, and each row sums to 2ⁿ.

lessons.title: Inclusion–Exclusion
lessons.summary: Count overlapping sets via |A∪B| = |A| + |B| − |A∩B|, then count poker hands.

lessons.title: The Pigeon Hole Principle
lessons.summary: When items outnumber containers, some container must repeat — guarantee existence with ⌈N/H⌉.

lessons.title: Counting Probabilities
lessons.summary: Turn counts into odds — P(event) = favorable ÷ total — and rank poker hands by probability.

tagline: Count cleverly to avoid counting everything.
